rescue_reasoning: The policy_statement and verbatim_text explicitly propose a circular economy workforce program: "workforce development program for the circular economy (repair/reuse skills, job placement, entrepreneurship)" and "training for repair and reuse skills". Repair/reuse and circular businesses are Resource Efficiency mechanisms that reduce waste and upstream material-related emissions. The parent_statement further anchors this in waste reduction goals ("Aggressively pursue waste reduction..."). The gate failure (missing outcome token) appears to be a tokenization issue rather than lack of climate/resource-efficiency relevance. Climate screen is explicit_self, so retain as climate-relevant under Resource Efficiency.
policy_statement: Offer a workforce development program for the circular economy (repair/reuse skills, job placement, entrepreneurship), prioritizing low-income communities, youth, and communities of color and publishing demographic participation data.
mechanism_description: Offer training, job placement, and entrepreneurship support for repair and reuse work to grow circular-economy activity and reduce waste and associated emissions.
primary_causal_pathway: Same service (consumer goods utility) → ↑ repair/reuse/recirculation → ↓ virgin material production + ↓ waste disposal → ↓ lifecycle GHG emissions
causal_mechanism_detail:
(1) Change in activity/system: Municipal programs train and place workers (and support entrepreneurs) in repair, refurbishment, reuse, and related circular-economy services, increasing local capacity and uptake of these services.  
(2) Mediating physical process: Extending product lifetimes and increasing reuse reduces throughput of materials, which (a) displaces energy- and emissions-intensive extraction/processing/manufacturing of virgin materials and (b) decreases quantities sent to landfill/incineration, lowering associated methane and combustion emissions.  
(3) Climate-relevant outcome: Net reduction in lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions (often dominated by avoided upstream manufacturing; secondarily by avoided landfill methane/incineration).
dominant_pathway_test: The single biggest climate-relevant effect is reduced lifecycle GHG emissions by increasing repair/reuse activity that avoids virgin material production and reduces waste disposal.
mechanism_classification_reasoning:
1) Dominant causal pathway: Workforce development → more repair/reuse/circular activity → lower material throughput → avoided virgin production and reduced disposal → lower lifecycle GHG emissions.  
2) Why this primary over alternatives: While the outcome is mitigation (GHG reduction), the mechanism operates chiefly through using fewer materials/keeping products in service longer (efficiency/circularity) rather than directly switching energy supply or capturing emissions; thus Resource Efficiency best matches the dominant pathway.  
3) Edge cases applied: If a program were primarily about waste diversion to reduce landfill methane (without a strong material displacement effect), Mitigation could dominate; however, the canonical mechanism is explicitly circular-economy repair/reuse workforce expansion, which is fundamentally material-efficiency driven.  
4) Applies to ALL instances: Any policy that builds repair/reuse workforce capacity aims to expand circular activity and reduce material demand and disposal; the primary category remains Resource Efficiency across contexts, even if co-benefits include jobs, equity, and local economic development.  
5) Secondary subtype deferred: Whether emissions reductions come more from avoided manufacturing vs. avoided landfill methane/incineration, and the magnitude, depends on local waste composition, grid intensity, and program design—this is deferred to policy-level evidence.
mechanism_edge_case_notes: If training is mainly for recycling processing (not repair/reuse) or primarily targets landfill diversion/organics management, the dominant pathway could shift toward direct waste-sector mitigation; for repair/reuse-centered circular workforce programs, Resource Efficiency remains the best primary fit.
key_indicators: "Offer a workforce development program"; "includes training for repair and reuse skills"; "job placement"; "entrepreneurship"; "prioritizing low-income communities, youth, and communities of color"; "publishing demographic participation data"
co_benefits: Workforce/economic development; equity and inclusion outcomes; reduced local pollution and landfill burdens (potential public health benefits).
instance_edge_case_notes: Includes an information/disclosure element (“publishing demographic participation data”), but the core instrument is programmatic support (training/placement/entrepreneurship), so it is classified primarily as an incentive-program.
